content_en:
Tabsirat al-Adillah's treatment of prophethood follows the standard kalam framework while incorporating some emphases distinctive to the Maturidi tradition. Abu Muin an-Nasafi establishes the rational possibility and the historical reality of prophethood, addresses the confirmation of prophethood through miracles, and examines the specific prophethood of Muhammad.

On the confirmation of Muhammad's prophethood, Abu Muin an-Nasafi gives particular attention to the Quran as the primary miracle. His treatment of Quranic inimitability (i'jaz) engages with both the linguistic and content dimensions of the argument, drawing on the tradition established by al-Baqillani and others while incorporating his own analysis. He also discusses the historical transmission of reports about Muhammad's prophethood — the mutawatir transmission that gives the basic facts of Muhammad's life and mission the status of certain rather than merely probable knowledge.

On the Maturidi school's theological method more broadly, Abu Muin an-Nasafi is explicit about what makes kalam a legitimate and necessary enterprise. Against traditionalists who rejected kalam as innovation, he argues that the obligation to defend the faith against philosophical and sectarian attack requires the tools of systematic rational theology. The prophetic mission itself requires that its truth be demonstrable — not merely asserted — so that doubters can be rationally persuaded. Kalam serves this function.

He is equally explicit about the limits of kalam: it is a tool for defending the faith, not a replacement for the direct religious knowledge that comes through revelation, hadith transmission, and genuine spiritual practice. The kalam scholar who masters all the arguments but lacks genuine faith and piety has the means without the end. This acknowledgment of kalam's instrumental rather than final status is characteristic of the Maturidi tradition's self-understanding.

The section on community (jama'ah) and on the status of Muslim sinners reflects the Maturidi tradition's careful navigation between the Khariji position that major sinners are unbelievers and the Murji'i position that sins do not affect faith. A major sinner remains a Muslim but a sinful one — their faith is real, their sinfulness is real, and both truths must be maintained without collapsing either into the other.
content_id:
Perlakuan Tabsirat al-Adillah terhadap kenabian mengikuti kerangka kalam standar sambil menggabungkan beberapa penekanan yang khas dari tradisi Maturidi. Abu Muin an-Nasafi menetapkan kemungkinan rasional dan realitas historis kenabian, membahas konfirmasi kenabian melalui mukjizat, dan mengkaji kenabian Muhammad secara khusus.

Mengenai konfirmasi kenabian Muhammad, Abu Muin an-Nasafi memberikan perhatian khusus pada Al-Quran sebagai mukjizat utama. Pembahasannya tentang kemukjizatan Al-Quran (ijaz) melibatkan dimensi linguistik maupun isi dari argumen tersebut, berlandaskan tradisi yang ditetapkan oleh al-Baqillani dan lainnya sambil menggabungkan analisisnya sendiri. Ia juga membahas transmisi historis laporan-laporan tentang kenabian Muhammad — transmisi mutawatir yang memberikan fakta-fakta dasar kehidupan dan misi Muhammad status pengetahuan yang pasti, bukan sekadar pengetahuan yang bersifat kemungkinan.

Mengenai metode teologis mazhab Maturidi secara lebih luas, Abu Muin an-Nasafi secara eksplisit menjelaskan apa yang menjadikan kalam sebagai sebuah disiplin yang sah dan diperlukan. Melawan para tradisionalis yang menolak kalam sebagai inovasi terlarang, ia berargumen bahwa kewajiban membela akidah dari serangan filosofis dan sektarian memerlukan perangkat teologi rasional yang sistematis. Misi kenabian itu sendiri mengharuskan kebenarannya dapat dibuktikan — bukan sekadar dinyatakan — sehingga para peragua dapat diyakinkan secara rasional. Kalam menjalankan fungsi ini.

Ia sama eksplisitnya tentang batas-batas kalam: ia adalah alat untuk membela akidah, bukan pengganti pengetahuan agama langsung yang datang melalui wahyu, transmisi hadis, dan praktik spiritual yang tulus. Ulama kalam yang menguasai semua argumen tetapi tidak memiliki keimanan dan kesalehan yang sejati memiliki sarana tanpa tujuan. Pengakuan atas status kalam yang bersifat instrumental alih-alih final ini merupakan ciri khas pemahaman diri tradisi Maturidi.

Bagian tentang komunitas (jamaah) dan status para pelaku dosa besar di kalangan Muslim mencerminkan navigasi cermat tradisi Maturidi antara posisi Khariji bahwa pelaku dosa besar adalah kafir dan posisi Murjiah bahwa dosa tidak mempengaruhi iman. Pelaku dosa besar tetap seorang Muslim tetapi seorang yang berdosa — keimanan mereka nyata, kedosaan mereka nyata, dan kedua kebenaran ini harus dipertahankan tanpa mengorbankan yang satu demi yang lain.
content_ar: Tabsirat al-Adillah's treatment of prophethood follows the standard علم الكلام framework بينما incorporating some emphases distinctive to the الماتريدي التراث. Abu Muin an-Nasafi establishes the rational possibility and the historical reality of prophethood, addresses the confirmation of prophethood through miracles, and examines the specific prophethood of محمد ﷺ. On the confirmation of محمد ﷺ's prophethood, Abu Muin an-Nasafi gives particular attention to the القرآن الكريم as the الأساسي miracle. His treatment of القرآني inimitability (i'jaz) engages with both the linguistic and content dimensions of the argument, drawing on the التراث established by al-Baqillani and others بينما incorporating his own analysis. He أيضاً discusses the historical transmission of reports about محمد ﷺ's prophethood — the mutawatir transmission that gives the الأساسي facts of محمد ﷺ's الحياة and mission the status of certain rather than merely probable العلم. On the الماتريدي school's theological method more broadly, Abu Muin an-Nasafi is explicit about what makes علم الكلام a legitimate and necessary enterprise. Against traditionalists who rejected علم الكلام as البدعة, he argues that the الالتزام to defend the الإيمان against philosophical and sectarian attack requires the tools of systematic rational theology. The prophetic mission itself requires that its الحق be demonstrable — not merely asserted — لذا that doubters can be rationally persuaded. علم الكلام serves this function. He is equally explicit about the limits of علم الكلام: it is a tool for defending the الإيمان, not a replacement for the direct religious العلم that comes through الوحي, الحديث transmission, and genuine spiritual practice. The علم الكلام scholar who masters all the arguments but lacks genuine الإيمان and piety has the means without the end. This acknowledgment of علم الكلام's instrumental rather than final status is characteristic of the الماتريدي التراث's self-الفهم. The الفصل on الجماعة (jama'ah) and on the status of مسلم sinners reflects the الماتريدي التراث's careful navigation between the Khariji position that major sinners are unbelievers and the Murji'i position that الذنوب do not affect الإيمان. A major sinner remains a مسلم but a sinful one — their الإيمان is real, their sinfulness is real, and both truths must be maintained without collapsing either into the other.
